data.title: Trump ends reciprocal tariffs on certain agricultural products
data.description:
• In an Executive Order issued Friday, President Trump has rolled back reciprocal tariffs on a host of agricultural products that were initially imposed in early April.
  • "I have received additional information and recommendations from various officials who, pursuant to my direction, have been monitoring the circumstances involving the emergency declared in Executive Order 14257," the new EO states.
  • "After considering the information and recommendations these officials have provided to me, the status of negotiations with various trading partners, current domestic demand for certain products, and current domestic capacity to produce certain products, among other things, I have determined that it is necessary and appropriate to further modify the scope of products subject to the reciprocal tariff..."
  • Among the agricultural products no longer subject to reciprocal tariffs are beef, coffee, many kinds of nuts, bananas, tomatoes, pineapples, and orange juice. Other exempt items include fuel oils, jet fuel, liquid/gaseous natural gas, and petroleum coke. 

More on tariffs

  • Trump eyes tariff relief for bananas, coffee and beef to reduce U.S. grocery bills

  • Why Trump's $2,000 Tariff Dividend Is The Last Thing The Economy Needs

  • Deal Hunt In The Global Food Sector Reignites In Wake Of Trump's Tariffs
  • Tariffs, not the Grinch, might steal Christmas: Tree prices seen rising
  • Trump warns paying back tariffs would be a $2T national security disaster

data.title: David Einhorn's DME Capital takes stakes in PG&E, exits Teck Resouces, among Q3 moves
data.description:
David Einhorn's DME Capital Management took a new stake in PG&E (PCG), United Parks &  Resorts (PRKS) and Nexstar Media (NXST) during the third quarter of the year, the hedge fund disclosed in a regulatory filing dated Friday.

Meanwhile, the firm, formerly known as Greenlight Capital, closed out its position in Teck Resources (TECK), according to the 13F filing.

The fund increased its stakes in Fluor (FLR), Victoria's Secret (VSCO), Acadia Health (ACHC) and Centene (CNC) in Q3. 

Einhorn's fund decreased its stake in HP Inc. (HPQ), CNH Industrial (CNH), Roivant Sciences (ROIV), Capri Holdings (CPRI) and Penn Entertainment  (PENN).

data.title: Elemental Royalty Corporation targets $42M revenue amid EMX merger and portfolio expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Elemental Royalty Corporation (ELE:CA) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Frederick Augustus Ronald Peter Bell opened by stating this is the last quarterly call as Elemental Altus Royalties, following the close of the merger with EMX Royalty. He highlighted, “it's the strongest financial position in the company's history, really good continuing cash build.” Bell reported Q3 adjusted revenue of $8.2 million, compared to $4.8 million in Q3 2024, and noted the merger creates a “USD 1 billion market cap royalty company, a portfolio of over 200 assets and 4 really cornerstone royalties.” He also announced two acquisitions in September: the Dugbe royalty (closed) and the Laverton royalty in Australia (expected to close this quarter). Bell detailed, “about USD 100 million of non-dilutive capital…available to deploy from today,” and confirmed the closing of a $100 million financing with Tether as part of the EMX merger.
  • CFO David Baker called the EMX merger “a genuinely transformative transaction for both companies,” outlining that the combined entity manages “more than 200 royalties with a larger base flowing and near cash flowing assets.” Baker stated, “the merger really does represent a clear step change for Elemental and really positions us firmly in the intermediate royalty space with enhanced access to growth and capital.” He described the Laverton and Dugbe acquisitions, highlighting Laverton as “one of the largest undeveloped gold systems in Australia,” and Dugbe as “one of the largest undeveloped gold deposits in West Africa.”

Outlook

  • Management is on track to meet the midpoint of Gold Equivalent Ounce (GEO) guidance, with a range of 11,600 to 13,200 GEO, and increased record adjusted revenue guidance of approximately $42 million, based on a Q4 gold price of $4,000. Bell clarified, “we are on the midpoint of guidance range for that, but we are increasing the revenue guidance on the basis of using a higher gold price in the Q4 quarter.”
  • Management expects growth from three key assets: Timok moving into the Lower Zone, the mine expansion at Karlawinda, and the Laverton project entering Genesis' mine plan.

Financial Results

  • Q3 royalty revenue was $6.9 million; adjusted revenue was $8.2 million, up 70% year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA reached $6.2 million, also up nearly 70% from Q3 2024. Adjusted cash flows from operations totaled $5 million, up nearly 80% from the prior year’s quarter.
  • Q3 gold equivalent ounces (GEO) were 2,362, a 22% increase from Q3 2024. Korali-Sud royalty contributed about $1 million of revenue in Q3 and over $10 million year-to-date. The pro forma balance sheet post-merger shows approximately $48 million, with a $50 million credit facility available.
  • Free cash flow for the year was $5.2 million. One-off items included costs related to the EMX merger and initial payments for the Laverton and Dugbe royalties. The company received $1.9 million from a partial royalty buyback at Arizona Sonoran Parks/Salyer and $2.2 million from share option exercises.

Q&A

  • An analyst asked if EMX Q3 financials would be available. CFO Baker responded that “EMX is now delisted, so we'll not be putting out its Q3s,” but a summary will be provided at year-end.
  • An analyst inquired about G&A expense synergies post-merger. CEO Bell answered, “we're expecting multimillion dollar synergies...the teams are currently working through that at the moment, and we will have a budget for the 2026 year that will be agreed.”
  • Regarding new paying royalties, Baker stated, “the Chapi mine in Peru is expected to restart production near-term,” and listed Gediktepe and Western Queen as other sources of expected new royalty revenue.
  • On acquisition strategy, Bell indicated, “as we sit here today, I think on consensus now numbers, Elemental Royalty Corp. as a combined entity is actually trading at a better valuation, a lower valuation than any of our peers in the space. So actually, the best value company we would acquire today would be ourselves.”
  • In response to Tether’s AlphaStream option, Baker confirmed, “Tether have exercised their option to acquire the shares from AlphaStream. That was completed premerger.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral and focused on integration, synergies, and acquisition strategy, with no overt skepticism or negativity evident in the questions.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, frequently referencing the company’s strong financial position and the transformative impact of the EMX merger. Bell’s statement, “I think all 3 of those are really material upgrades in terms of revenue from some of our cornerstone assets,” signaled optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence has increased, particularly due to the merger and expanded growth opportunities. Analysts’ sentiment remained consistent, with attention shifting toward integration details and forward guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue and adjusted EBITDA rose sharply compared to the prior quarter, though Q2 had higher adjusted revenue and cash flow, likely reflecting timing of milestone payments. The company increased revenue guidance from $35-$40 million (Q2) to $42 million (Q3), driven by higher gold prices and portfolio expansion.
  • Strategic focus shifted from organic cash build to transformative growth through the EMX merger and acquisitions. Analysts’ questions in Q3 emphasized integration and cost synergies, differing from Q2’s focus on liquidity, credit lines, and listing plans.
  • Management’s tone in Q3 was notably more bullish about growth prospects, compared to the more cautious optimism of Q2.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted a weather-related delay in Q3 sales at Caserones, with expectations of recovery in Q4.
  • Royalty payments from Wahgnion remain paused due to an external audit by the government of Burkina Faso. Management will update when more information is available.
  • One-off costs related to the EMX merger and new acquisitions impacted free cash flow in Q3.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that the EMX merger establishes Elemental Royalty Corporation as a $1 billion royalty company with a diversified portfolio of over 200 assets and significant liquidity for future acquisitions. With upgraded revenue guidance of $42 million and a strong balance sheet, the company is positioned for continued growth through both organic asset performance and strategic deal-making, supported by the combined expertise of Elemental Altus and EMX teams and the backing of a major investor, Tether.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SA Asks: How will retailers fare this holiday season?
data.description:
How will retailers fare this holiday season amid increased tariffs, persistent inflation, and heightened concern about the state of the U.S. economy?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts APAC Investment News, Daniel Jones, and Daniel Javier who they think the winners and losers might be this holiday season in retail. 

APAC Investment News: Just as we're experiencing a K-shaped economy, I suspect we'll see a K-shaped holiday season, with wealthier households generally spending profligately while lower-income households tighten their belts. Luxury retailers like LVMH (OTCPK:LVMHF) (OTCPK:LVMUY) and Hermes (OTCPK:HESAY) (OTCPK:HESAF) should do particularly well.

I believe this year we'll see especially aggressive deal hunting. I'd be careful not to get lured into false confidence, especially due to high engagement on Black Friday and Cyber Monday. A busy Thanksgiving shopping holiday may portend aggressive deal hunting, not stronger overall holiday shopping.

Amazon (AMZN) and Walmart (WMT) are hard to beat price-wise. Ditto for Costco (COST). Target (TGT) is in the middle of a tough turnaround, and I believe they'll try to make a statement and woo back customers with cheap deals. Also, I believe the Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOY) Switch 2 will rank among the most popular gifts.

I don't think the holidays will be as bad as the most cynical economic indicators might suggest, but I suspect people will lean into using credit, which might cause problems later. Retailers may enjoy a share price bump throughout December, but I believe that’ll cool off come the new year.

Daniel Jones: This is going to be a really interesting holiday season. With consumer confidence levels near all-time lows, employment data looking awful, drama associated with the government shutdown in the U.S., and the negative effects stemming from tariffs, it's likely that retailers will face challenges this season. However, there are some that might do surprisingly well. 

Even though I am bearish about the company because of how expensive shares are, Winmark Corporation (WINA) operates a franchise business model that focuses on reselling used items. This is a value-driven enterprise, and with consumers likely to pinch pennies this season, more than at perhaps any time in years, I could fully expect its revenue to come in stronger than what you might think likely. 

Another retailer that is probably going to do quite well is video game retailer GameStop (GME). When it announced its last quarterly results, it posted some strong growth in key areas. This was likely because of the successful Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOY) Switch 2. Earlier this month, Nintendo announced that it was increasing its sales forecast for this gaming console to 19 million units for the fiscal year ending in March of 2026. That compares to the previous forecast of 15 million units. The sale of these consoles, as well as related accessories, could prove positive for GameStop. 

Discount play Five Below (FIVE) also looks likely to do well. Its stock price is up 83% this year alone, thanks to strong fundamentals and continued store growth. This optimism comes from my overall thesis of a weakening economy that will push customers to focus more on cheaper items. 

I would venture to say retailers that fall outside the value category and the niche space, like GameStop (GME), which is benefiting from a catalyst, will probably suffer. 

Perhaps the most negatively affected will be furniture retailers. A great example of this can be seen by looking at Hooker Furnishings (HOFT), which has seen its share price drop 46.6% year to date. Even though furniture sales have so far been up this year, a weakening economy will not bode well for this industry. And with this business already experiencing a decline in revenue, profits, and cash flows, despite recent growth in the space, it should be particularly susceptible.

Daniel Javier: Despite stubborn inflation, many retailers may still pull off a good performance this holiday season. The 50-bps rate cut may boost economic activity and labor market conditions. It can also make credit cards more attractive to support holiday spending. Here are my top stocks. 

1. Costco Wholesale (COST) may remain inflation-resilient with its membership model. Consumers aim to get more value for their money and do bulk buying. 

2. Amazon (AMZN) is another good contender as e-commerce remains solid. Its vast streamlined logistics and global footprint may help manage cost pressures amid new tariffs. Lastly, it can capitalize on AWS for more efficient inventory and pricing management and on Prime to increase membership. 

3. Walmart's (WMT) low-price business model itself is already a value proposition. Its gradual e-commerce growth and pick-up/delivery options may capture demand. 

4. Home Depot (HD) is also strategic with its high concentration in states with the largest number of large corporations and high-net-worth individuals, such as California, Florida, New York, and Texas. It may also benefit from the interest rate cut and decreasing mortgage rates due to positive spillovers into the property market. 

5. Puregold Price Club (OTCPK:PGCMF) is like a fusion of COST and WMT in the Philippines. But it prudently segments its target market with its two big brands, namely, Puregold and S&R. Puregold targets lower-income and lower-middle-income households with its low-price business model and redeemable points per transaction. Meanwhile, S&R uses a membership model, which targets inflation-resistant buyers coming from middle-income, upper-middle-income, and rich households.

  • Top Home Improvement Retail Stocks

data.title: TILT holdings announces pending delisting from Cboe Canada
data.description:
• TILT Holdings (OTCQB:TLLTF) (TILT:CA) said on Friday that its shares were suspended from trading on Cboe Canada on November 7, 2025, after the company received a letter from Cboe notifying it of the suspension following creditor protection under Canada’s Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act.
  • The company’s securities were also halted on the OTCID marketplace operated by OTC Markets Group.
  • TILT said its common shares will be officially delisted from Cboe Canada on November 18, and there is no assurance that the trading suspension will be lifted prior to the delisting.
  • TLLTF closed +11.29% at $0.0069.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Jana Partners exits Wex, takes new stake in Six Flags Entertainment among other Q3 moves
data.description:
Jana Partners, the activist hedge fund founded by billionaire Barry Rosenstein, took a new position in Six Flags Entertainment (FUN), decreased stake in Trimble (TRMB), while closed out its position in Wex (WEX) in its third quarter moves.

Jana disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing, which was published on Friday.

The firm boosted its holdings in Rapid7 (RPD) to 6.5M shares from 5.73M shares and in Freshpet (FRPT) to 1.05M shares from 1M shares.

Meanwhile, it decreased its stake in Trimble to 1.8M shares from 3.6M shares, in Lamb Weston (LW) to 5M shares from 7.2M shares and in Mercury Systems (MRCY) to 5.9M from 6.7M shares. The firm also lowered its stake in Markel Group (MKL).

It opened new stakes in The Cooper Companies (COO) with 2.43M shares and in Alkami Technology (ALKT) with 1.89M shares, the filing showed.

data.title: Rivalry extends closing date for non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Rivalry (RVLY:CA) (OTCQX: RVLCF) (FSE: 9VK) said on Friday it has extended by 30 days the closing date for the final tranche of its previously announced non-brokered private placement.
  • The company said all terms of the financing remain unchanged and it expects to complete the final tranche on or before December 15.
  • Rivalry intends to use the proceeds for corporate development and general working capital.
  • The company reiterated that the securities have not been registered under U.S. securities laws and cannot be sold in the United States without registration or an applicable exemption.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pershing Square reduces stake in Alphabet, Uber, among Q3 moves
data.description:
Bill Ackman's Pershing Square reduced its stake in Alphabet  (GOOGL) and Uber (UBER) ,among other moves in Q3.

The fund disclosed the information in its latest  13 Filing for the three months ended September 30, 2025, published on Friday.

The fund also reduced its stakes in Restaurant Brands (QSR) and Brookfield Corp.

data.title: ValueAct open new stake in Toast, dumps Redfin, Expedia in Q3 buys/sells
data.description:
ValueAct took a new position is Toast (TOST), with 3.4M shares, during the third quarter of the year, the hedge fund disclosed on Friday.

On the flip side, the firm exited its stakes in Redfin (RDFN) and Expedia (EXPE).

Increased holdings include Meta Platforms (META), to 0.77M shares in Q3 from 0.62M shares in the prior quarter, and Visa (V), to 1.57M from 1.24M in Q2.

It reduced its positions in Walt Disney (DIS), to 4.39M shares in Q3 from 5.09M shares in the earlier three-month period, and Roblox (RBLX), to 4.28M from 4.73M in Q2, according to the filing.
